title: T-038 — UI: TEC-021 BOM Detail page with 7 tabs
prompt: Build /technical/boms/[id] page (TEC-021, §7.5) with tabs: Ingredients Tree / Cost Panel / Routing / Process Params / Costs / Versions / Graph / Recipe Sheet — matching prototype tab structure. Match `bom_detail_page` design/Monopilot Design System/technical/bom-detail.jsx:3-60 (page chrome) plus the seven tab components in same file lines 63-603. RED tests/ui/tec-021-bom-detail.test.tsx. Run: `pnpm vitest run tests/ui/tec-021-bom-detail.test.tsx`.

pipeline_inputs.description: BOM detail with 7 tabs (Ingredients Tree, Cost Panel, Routing, Process Params, Costs, Versions, Graph, Recipe Sheet).
pipeline_inputs.details: Each tab is a Client/Server Component split. Versions tab links to T-040 diff view. Graph tab uses lightweight DAG renderer (e.g., reactflow) — keep behind dynamic import.
acceptance_criteria: Given the BOM detail renders, when compared to design/Monopilot Design System/technical/bom-detail.jsx:3-60 and the seven tab components at lines 63-152, 154-206, 209-249, 252-307, 310-370, 373-468, 471-544, 551-603, then tab order, header, and primitives match — structural + visual + interaction parity verified by RTL counting tabs and asserting layout sections.
Given user clicks 'Versions' tab, when fired, then version timeline renders and includes a 'View diff' link per pair.
Given user lacks technical.bom.approve, when on Versions tab, then 'Approve' action is hidden.
Given BOM has co_products, when on Ingredients Tree tab, then they are rendered with byproduct flag.
test_strategy: RED tests for parity + tab order + RBAC.
risk_red_lines: Do not collapse tabs into a single accordion.
